title: The Deeper Implications of Your Favorite split
display_title: The Deeper Implications of Your Favorite split
wikitext:
[]: Post by Xard » Thu Aug 04, 2011 5:04 pm Anonymous 08/04/11(Thu)17:50 No.52895345 >>52895241 Reifags are the ones with the mother issues. Kaworufags are fatgirls and ronery homos. Asukafags are retards who like flashy things. Misatobros are men of good taste and refinement.

[]: Post by CJD » Thu Aug 04, 2011 6:45 pm Xard wrote: Asukafags are retards who like flashy things.

[]: Post by C.A.P. » Thu Aug 04, 2011 7:12 pm I drink alcohol? Also, I'm going to use that first Misatobro comment now. I like the sound of that word - makes me sounds like a manly nerd whose own existence is a living contradiction of time and space.

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Thu Aug 04, 2011 8:10 pm Xard wrote: Discuss

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Aug 04, 2011 9:05 pm Anonymous 08/04/11(Thu)17:50 No.52895345 >>52895241 Reifags are the ones with the mother issues. Kaworufags are fatgirls and ronery homos. Asukafags are retards who like flashy things. Misatobros are men of good taste and refinement.

[]: Post by CJD » Thu Aug 04, 2011 9:09 pm Tarnsman wrote: I like Asuka because I have a serious fetish for emotionally broken girls with no self esteem and long to take advantage of her issues for my own sick goals. Unfortunately she is a cartoon so I am forever alone.

[]: Post by Allemann » Fri Aug 05, 2011 3:07 am Misatobros are men of good taste and refinement.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 05, 2011 3:19 am CJD wrote: A la, Asuka fans are real life Shinji's.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 05, 2011 8:53 am CJD wrote: Bro's before Ho's man, bro's before ho's.

[]: Post by C.A.P. » Fri Aug 05, 2011 11:04 am Allemann wrote: This is correct, as Misato is the closest to the male conception of the female ideal.

[]: Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Fri Aug 05, 2011 11:11 am CJD wrote: A la, Asuka fans are real life Shinji's.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 05, 2011 11:20 am AngelNo13Bardiel wrote: ...well no shit, I figured that one out some years ago. Then again, it is always nice to get a second opinion.

[]: Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Fri Aug 05, 2011 12:53 pm Bagheera wrote: Avoidant types appreciate people who'll draw them out of their shells, even if the process is painful. Better that than sweet words that reinforce your habits and don't ultimately leave you any better off than before.

[]: Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 05, 2011 1:18 pm Bagheera wrote: Avoidant types appreciate people who'll draw them out of their shells, even if the process is painful. Better that than sweet words that reinforce your habits and don't ultimately leave you any better off than before.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 05, 2011 1:29 pm Reichu wrote: I'm an avoidant personality type, but my sense of personal space and self-respect works against wanting anything overtly resembling Asuka sticking its nose in my business.

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Fri Aug 05, 2011 2:02 pm Bagheera wrote: Good. No one with this sort of attitude has any business being with another human being. Stick to cartoons and let real people dodge the bullet.

[]: Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 05, 2011 2:16 pm Tarnsman wrote: That's mean, everyone has the right to happiness.

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 05, 2011 2:27 pm Reichu wrote: I'm an avoidant personality type, but my sense of personal space and self-respect works against wanting anything overtly resembling Asuka sticking its nose in my business. "I'm an idiot, huh? Time to look a bit more closely next time you're preening in the mirror, hon."

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Fri Aug 05, 2011 2:38 pm Bagheera wrote: I didn't say we want it. I said we appreciate it (well, those who are aware of their pathology and unhappy with it, anyway). Better someone who harasses you and gets you to do something other than hiding out in your apartment for days on end, eh?

[]: Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 05, 2011 2:40 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: It's nice to read that I'm not the only one around here holding this idea.
